title: olearia stricta
intro: Olearia stricta, also known as the New Zealand tree daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of rheumatic and inflammatory conditions, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including the relief of pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing. Olearia stricta is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and creams, and is also used in traditional Maori medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
